Same as `fast_keccak`, but it's a little more optimal calling `hexdigest()`

functions.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.safe.utils.fast_bytes_to_checksum_address
documentation:
Converts to checksum_address. Uses more optimal `pysha3` instead of `eth_utils` for keccak256 calculation.
As input is already in bytes, some checks and conversions can be skipped, providing a speedup of ~50%

```
param value



return

```

methods.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.safe.safe.Safe.build_multisig_tx
documentation:
Allows to execute a Safe transaction confirmed by required number of owners and then pays the account
that submitted the transaction. The fees are always transfered, even if the user transaction fails

```
param to

Destination address of Safe transaction

param value

Ether value of Safe transaction

param data

Data payload of Safe transaction

param operation

Operation type of Safe transaction

param safe_tx_gas

Gas that should be used for the Safe transaction

param base_gas

Gas costs for that are independent of the transaction execution
(e.g. base transaction fee, signature check, payment of the refund)

param gas_price

Gas price that should be used for the payment calculation

param gas_token

Token address (or  if ETH) that is used for the payment

param refund_receiver

Address of receiver of gas payment (or  if tx.origin).

param signatures

Packed signature data ({bytes32 r}{bytes32 s}{uint8 v})

param safe_nonce

Nonce of the safe (to calculate hash)

return

SafeTx
```

methods.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.safe.safe_tx.SafeTx.__init__
documentation:
---
param base_gas: true
param data: true
param gas_price: true
param gas_token: true
param operation: true
param refund_receiver: true
param safe_address: true
param safe_nonce: Current nonce of the Safe. If not provided, it will be retrieved
  from network
param safe_tx_gas: true
param safe_version: Safe version 1.0.0 renamed `baseGas` to `dataGas`. Safe version
  1.3.0 added `chainId` to
param signatures: true
param to: true
param value: true
param w3: true
---

the `domainSeparator`. If not provided, it will be retrieved from network
\:param chain_id: Ethereum network chain_id is used in hash calculation for Safes >= 1.3.0. If not provided,
it will be retrieved from the provided ethereum_client

methods.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.safe.safe_tx.SafeTx.execute
documentation:
Send multisig tx to the Safe

```
param tx_sender_private_key

Sender private key

param tx_gas

Gas for the external tx. If not,  will be used

param tx_gas_price

Gas price of the external tx. If not,  will be used

param tx_nonce

Force nonce for 

param block_identifier

 or 

param eip1559_speed

If provided, use EIP1559 transaction

return

Tuple(tx_hash, tx)

raises

InvalidMultisigTx: If user tx cannot go through the Safe
```

functions.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.safe.eip712.encode_data
documentation:
Encode structured data as per Ethereum's signTypeData_v4.

https://docs.metamask.io/guide/signing-data.html#sign-typed-data-v4

This code is ported from the Javascript "eth-sig-util" package.

functions.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.safe.eip712.eip712_encode
documentation:
Given a dict of structured data and types, return a 3-element tuple of
the encoded, signable data.

  0: The magic & version (0x1901)
  1: The encoded types
  2: The encoded data

functions.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.safe.eip712.eip712_encode_hash
documentation:
---
param typed_data: EIP712 structured data and types
return: Keccak256 hash of encoded signable data
---

classes.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.maker
documentation: Maker of the order, i.e the source of funds for the order

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.taker
documentation: Address of the order taker. The zero address is used to indicate a public order

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.tokenId
documentation:
Token Id of the CTF ERC1155 asset to be bought or sold.
If BUY, this is the tokenId of the asset to be bought, i.e the makerAssetId
If SELL, this is the tokenId of the asset to be sold, i.e the  takerAssetId

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.makerAmount
documentation: Maker amount, i.e the max amount of tokens to be sold

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.takerAmount
documentation: Taker amount, i.e the minimum amount of tokens to be received

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.side
documentation: The side of the order, BUY or SELL

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.feeRateBps
documentation: Fee rate, in basis points, charged to the order maker, charged on proceeds

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.nonce
documentation: Nonce used for onchain cancellations

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.signer
documentation: Signer of the order. Optional, if it is not present the signer is the maker of the order.

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.expiration
documentation:
Timestamp after which the order is expired.
Optional, if it is not present the value is '0' (no expiration)

class_variables.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.py_order_utils.model.order.OrderData.signatureType
documentation: Signature type used by the Order. Default value 'EOA'
Inputs to generate orders

---
param safe_hash: Signed hash for the Safe (message or transaction)
param signature: Owner signature
---

methods.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.safe.safe_signature.SafeSignature.parse_signature
documentation:
---
param ignore_trailing: |-
  Ignore trailing data on the signature. Some libraries pad it and add some zeroes at
  the end
param safe_hash: Signed hash for the Safe (message or transaction)
param safe_hash_preimage: '`safe_hash` preimage for EIP1271 validation'
param signatures: One or more signatures appended. EIP1271 data at the end is supported.
return: List of SafeSignatures decoded
---

methods.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.safe.safe_signature.SafeSignature.export_signatures
documentation:
Takes a list of SafeSignature objects and exports them as a valid signature for the contract

```
param safe_signatures



return

Valid signature for the Safe contract
```

methods.name: olab_prediction_market_sdk.chain.safe.safe_signature.SafeSignature.export_signature
documentation:
Exports signature in a format that's valid individually. That's important for contract signatures, as it
will fix the offset

:return:

documentation:
---
return: Decode owner from signature, without any further validation (signature can
  be not valid)
---

---

  Address of contract signing. No further checks to get the owner are needed,

documentation:
Fix offset (s) and append `contract_signature` at the end of the signature

:return:
